Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 13:32 ET
Index puts clash with single-name calls as negative gamma amplifies NFP positioning
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. The P/C divergence (index 1.67 vs single-name 0.87) signals institutional dispersion hedges ahead of Friday's NFP print.
  • 2. Massive AMD call blocks totaling 40,000 contracts contradict the bearish technical death cross, suggesting informed positioning.
  • 3. Negative $16.4B SPY gamma creates explosive risk into Friday's jobs data with gamma flip at $659.33.
NARRATIVE — NFP event risk positioning

The most striking pattern is the structural divergence between index and single-name options flow, with institutions loading index puts (1.67 P/C) while accumulating single-name calls (0.87 P/C) — a classic dispersion hedge ahead of Friday's NFP. This positioning makes sense given the 0.31 implied correlation reading, which signals stock-specific opportunities even if the index disappoints on jobs data. AMD's massive call accumulation defies its bearish technical setup, with 40,000 contracts across May 185C and June 180C strikes totaling $62.68M in premium. The flow contradicts ARM's death cross signal and suggests institutional conviction despite the technical breakdown, potentially driven by AI server demand expectations that aren't reflected in chart patterns. The negative gamma environment amplifies all positioning risks into Friday's NFP, with SPY's -$16.4B dealer gamma requiring constant hedging that accelerates any directional move. The 30.7 VIX in backwardation warns of elevated near-term event risk, while 10 names showing IV backwardation including AMD and META confirm broad pre-event positioning across mega-caps.
